CEO Tim Cook called the increases unavoidable, signaling that enterprise hardware refresh budgets will absorb the same memory-pricing pressure already squeezing the broader device market.

title: IBM Unveils New Method to Make Smaller Computer Chip Parts
blurb: IBM introduced a new fabrication method for building smaller transistor components, addressing industry concerns that chip miniaturization had hit a physical wall. Continued density gains keep the cost-per-compute curve bending downward, which matters to enterprise buyers planning multi-year hardware refreshes and the AI infrastructure roadmaps that depend on cheaper, denser silicon from suppliers like IBM's foundry partners.

title: Micron inks long-term supply agreements worth $100 billion — says it has no idea when RAM crisis will end
blurb: Micron signed 16 strategic customer agreements, 14 of which lock in roughly $100B in minimum baseline revenue over five-year terms through 2030, plus $22B in upfront cash deposits and binding financial commitments. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra said memory supply shortages will persist into 2027 and improve only gradually in 2028, signaling sustained price pressure on DRAM and NAND for enterprise hardware buyers.

title: New macOS malware embeds fake errors to confuse AI analysis tools
blurb: SentinelOne identified a North Korean-linked macOS malware family it named Gaslight, a Rust backdoor that embeds 38 fabricated system-failure messages as prompt injection to make LLM-assisted analysis tools doubt their own session and abort. The technique targets the AI triage agent's perception rather than the sandbox, signaling that security teams adopting AI-assisted reverse engineering now need safeguards against prompt injection in the samples they analyze.

title: Amazon, Microsoft’s Cloud Services Should Fall Under DMA, EU Says
blurb: The EU's competition regulators concluded that Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure should be designated as gatekeepers under the Digital Markets Act, subjecting their cloud businesses to interoperability and anti-self-preferencing rules. A formal designation would force the two largest cloud providers to ease customer lock-in and data portability, reshaping procurement leverage for enterprises that run on AWS and Azure.

title: Construction of 'world-changing' national supercomputer begins at Edinburgh University
blurb: Construction began on the UK's next national supercomputer at the University of Edinburgh, backed by a £750M government investment and built to deliver at least an exaflop, roughly 50 times the throughput of the current ARCHER2 system. Hosted by UK Research and Innovation, the machine targets research workloads in drug discovery, aircraft engineering, and climate modeling, with surplus heat redirected to warm university buildings.

title: Amazon ups India bet with fresh $13B AI infrastructure investment
blurb: Amazon committed an additional $13B to expand AWS data center capacity in Mumbai and Hyderabad through 2030, bringing its total India investment pledges to $48B. The move follows Microsoft's $17.5B and Google's $15B commitments, positioning India as a primary AWS region for enterprises that need in-country compute to satisfy data residency rules and New Delhi's tax incentives for locally run cloud workloads.

title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
blurb: Black Kite tracked 684 ransomware attacks across European organizations in the first four months of 2026, up 55% from the 441 recorded over the same span in 2025, as RaaS operators shift focus from an oversaturated US market. Researchers attribute the pivot to AI-assisted target research surfacing EU exposure, with European firms increasingly hit through compromised vendors rather than direct attacks.

title: Incode acquires Identiq, commits $100M to privacy-first identity tech
blurb: Incode acquired Identiq, an Israeli startup whose cryptographic tools let institutions confirm whether they have seen a fraudulent identity before without exposing customer records, and tied the deal to a $100M push into privacy-preserving identity infrastructure. For enterprise buyers, the bet is that fraud prevention improves by holding less customer data, with Incode planning on-device biometric processing and integration of Identiq's tech across billions of verifications a year.

title: Exclusive: LucidLink launches MCP server to give AI agents shared access to distributed files
blurb: LucidLink launched a public beta of its Model Context Protocol server, exposing its distributed streaming file system to any MCP-compatible agent or orchestrator, including Claude, OpenAI's Agents SDK, LangChain, LlamaIndex, and CrewAI. The server gives multi-agent systems a persistent, writable layer with shared state across clouds, on-premises, and edge environments, letting agents and human reviewers work from the same files without repeatedly copying or moving data between AI platforms.

title: Apple hikes prices for most of its hardware
blurb: Apple raised prices across most of its Mac and iPad lineup, with hikes ranging from $100 on the entry-level MacBook Neo and base iPad to $1,300 on the M3 Ultra Mac Studio, plus increases on Apple TV, HomePod, and Vision Pro. The move follows Tim Cook's warning that Apple can no longer absorb the memory-component crunch, and it raises hardware refresh costs for IT fleets while iPhone and AirPods pricing stays untouched.

title: Apple Store is down: ‘We’re making updates,’ Apple says [Update: Back online]
blurb: Apple's Online Store went offline this morning and returned with across-the-board price increases on Macs, iPads, and accessories, which Tim Cook had flagged last week as a response to rising component costs. For enterprise buyers running Apple fleets, the hikes raise hardware TCO ahead of the delayed Back to School promotion and coincide with new eligibility verification that closes the education-pricing honor system.

title: Elastic stretches workforce 7% thinner as AI does more of the heavy lifting
blurb: Elastic cut roughly 7% of its workforce, with CEO Ash Kulkarni framing the reduction as AI-enabled automation that lets the company run leaner engineering teams now organized into three core areas. The move signals that even infrastructure vendors selling search and observability tooling are restructuring their own headcount around AI productivity gains, a pattern enterprise buyers should expect to see reflected in vendor roadmaps and support models.

title: CIOs rethink the balance between AI oversight and innovation
blurb: CIOs are now their CEO's top AI mandate, tasked with scaling adoption fast while two-thirds of senior leaders report rising pressure to prove ROI, per Kyndryl's 2025 Readiness Report. The recurring advice from IT leaders is to embed governance as guardrails rather than gates, since only 31% feel ready to manage AI's external business risks. The harder shift is treating AI as a redesign of how work gets done, not automation of existing tasks.

title: Microsoft adds reusable skills and finance data connectors to Copilot in Excel
blurb: Microsoft added reusable Skills, finance data connectors, and a planning mode to Copilot in Excel, with prebuilt finance skills and partners including LSEG, Ramp, and Vena distributing more through the Microsoft Marketplace. New connectors for FactSet, Morningstar, PitchBook, and S&P Global may require a separate data-provider subscription, so a Microsoft 365 Copilot license alone will not unlock the full finance stack.

title: Palo Alto Networks, IBM to match patching with AI pace for operational threats and more
blurb: Palo Alto Networks and IBM expanded their alliance, integrating Palo Alto's Virtual Patching with IBM and Red Hat's $5B Project Lightwell to deliver same-day virtual patches at the network layer and validated open source fixes across OT, commercial, and healthcare software. The venture targets the shrinking gap between vulnerability disclosure and exploit, giving enterprises already running Palo Alto or Red Hat a bundled remediation path without standing up a separate patch-orchestration vendor.

title: Read Along in Google Classroom is now available to all education users to support foundational literacy
blurb: Google made Read Along generally available inside Google Classroom for all education users, adding real-time pronunciation support, phonics and comprehension analytics, and a library of hundreds of titles across eight languages, plus Gemini-assisted creation of differentiated reading activities. It ships enabled by default across Education Fundamentals, Standard, and Plus, with admin controls to disable it at the domain or OU level and full rollout expected by July 3.

title: Apple will skip M6 Pro and M6 Max chips, new report says
blurb: Apple will skip the Pro and Max variants of its M6 chip, shipping only the base M6 this year before pivoting to the M7 generation, according to a Bloomberg report. The move breaks a pattern in place since 2020 and pushes the next high-end Apple Silicon to late 2027, leaving workstation-class Mac buyers reliant on the forthcoming M5 Ultra Mac Studio in the interim.

title: Here are all of the Apple price hikes
blurb: Apple raised prices across most of its hardware lineup today, including iPads, Macs, the MacBook Neo, HomePods, and Vision Pro, with increases ranging from $30 on the HomePod mini to as much as $2,800 on high-memory MacBook Pro configurations. The hikes track surging RAM and SSD costs driven by AI data-center stockpiling, putting Apple alongside Nintendo, Sony, and Microsoft and signaling higher refresh budgets for any IT fleet standardized on Mac and iPad hardware.

title: CoreWeave enlists Backblaze for massive cloud storage expansion
blurb: CoreWeave signed a $335M, multi-exabyte deal with Backblaze to provide managed cloud storage capacity across its neocloud infrastructure, using Backblaze's B2 Neo tier built for GPU operators. The agreement adds to CoreWeave's $1.17B Vast Data commitment from November, layering lower-cost HDD-based storage beneath high-performance tiers to optimize data placement and preserve premium capacity for AI workloads.

title: How we built saga rollbacks for Cloudflare Workflows
blurb: Cloudflare shipped saga rollbacks for Workflows, letting developers declare compensation logic directly inside each step.do() call to semantically reverse completed steps when a later one fails. Each undo runs as its own durable, idempotent step that unwinds in reverse order, replacing the hand-rolled tracking code teams previously wrote to keep multi-step transactions from leaving partial state on failure.

title: Building a Cluster-Aware AI Agent with Kubernetes, Argo CD, and GitOps
blurb: A walkthrough builds a self-hosted, read-only AI agent that runs inside a Kubernetes cluster, observing live state through the API and reasoning with a local Mistral 7B model on Ollama, so no cluster data ever leaves the network. The agent's /diagnose endpoint grounds answers in real pod state, returning "api-7b8d restarted 14 times with ImagePullBackOff" instead of generic CrashLoopBackOff advice. A scoped ServiceAccount limits it to read-only access, while GitHub Actions and Argo CD Image Updater handle the GitOps delivery chain.

title: GitLab Patches Code Execution, Information Disclosure Vulnerabilities
blurb: GitLab shipped CE/EE security releases 19.1.1, 19.0.3, and 18.11.6 that patch 13 vulnerabilities, including three high-severity bugs: stored XSS in the EE Analytics dashboard and Web IDE workbench, plus an output-filtering flaw in Duo Workflows that could leak committed project data. GitLab.com already runs the fix, but self-managed operators should upgrade now, since the Web IDE XSS is exploitable by unauthenticated attackers.

title: AI agent identification service gets official nod from open source giant
blurb: The Linux Foundation launched the Agent Name Service, an open framework originally built by GoDaddy that extends DNS to identify, verify, and locate AI agents, with backing from Cisco, Cloudflare, and Salesforce. ANS ties agent identity and permissions to existing DNS infrastructure and folds in legal entity identifiers and decentralized identifiers, giving platform teams one verification model for an autonomous-agent attack surface that vendors like Palo Alto Networks have flagged as a fast-growing breach risk.

title: Zero-Downtime Deployments with Docker Compose – No Kubernetes Required
blurb: The StatusDude team argues that Kubernetes is unnecessary for serious production workloads, detailing how they run multi-region workers and thousands of monitoring checks per minute on Docker Compose and HAProxy with zero dropped requests. The post is largely a Traefik post-mortem: duplicate routing labels caused blanket 404s, scale-down races produced 502s as the routing table lagged Docker's state, and the absence of cross-backend retry was the dealbreaker. HAProxy's retry-on-different-backend behavior is what made true zero-downtime rolling deploys possible.

title: Optimizing cloud economics with linear elastic caching
blurb: Google researchers introduced linear elastic caching, an approach that frames cache eviction as a ski-rental problem and uses lightweight ML to dynamically size the cache against real-time workloads. By treating memory as a variable cost that integrates over both data size and hold time, the method targets the Goldilocks trade-off behind LRU's fixed allocation, cutting total cost of ownership where serverless providers charge up to $3 per GiB-day without degrading hit rates.

title: From Zero to $350,000: How Genni Built Scent Beauty’s TikTok Shop in 90 Days
blurb: Scent Beauty hit $350K in GMV and Star-Seller status within 90 days of launching on TikTok Shop with no prior presence, while drawing 800-plus creator applications a week. The agency behind it argues most brands misprice the channel by treating it as a bottom-funnel buy like Amazon when it functions as a discovery engine. At $7-9 CPM, 10M organic views carry roughly $70K in earned media value on top of $40-50K in affiliate GMV, so scoring TikTok Shop on sales alone undercounts the return.

title: The AI era requires a different kind of experimentation.
blurb: The author argues that the experimentation playbook most teams built in 2022 is now obsolete, because faster AI-era product development makes the old emphasis on minor surface tweaks, two-week win cycles, and pre/post measurement instead of true A/B tests a poor fit. The bigger limitation was always that those experiments mostly pulled forward revenue that would have landed anyway rather than driving step-function gains, so she recommends running bigger bets, testing foundational complexity and monetization, and skipping some tests entirely.

title: Elevate your campaign performance with June’s Demand Gen Drop.
blurb: Google rolled out its June Demand Gen Drop for Google Ads, adding broader video resizing across vertical, square, and landscape aspect ratios, Gemini-powered creative recommendations for YouTube assets, and web-to-app acquisition measurement for app installs. The measurement update lets marketers attribute new app users to Demand Gen campaigns, closing a gap that previously hid the full conversion value of YouTube spend.

title: Keeping Data-Driven Content Fresh Was a Monthly Slog. So We Taught an Agent to Do It.
blurb: Ahrefs built an internal agent, the Data Refresh Hub, to automate the monthly grind of updating its data-driven posts, pulling fresh numbers for 14 datasets from Keywords Explorer and Brand Radar, cleaning each by its own rules, and assembling a WordPress draft with new tables. The agent has run for two months and saves at least 20 hours per month, letting the team update posts monthly instead of quarterly and publish more of them. The takeaway is that the win comes from automating the tedious data work, not the writing.

title: Meta Relaunches Facebook Creator Studio as AI-Powered App
blurb: Meta is relaunching Facebook Creator Studio as a standalone AI app that learns a creator's voice, audience, and goals, surfaces daily priorities, and drafts in-voice comment replies for review before posting. The move arrives as Meta splits its Professional Dashboard into separate Creator and Business products and prepares to add Facebook creators to Creator Marketplace, joining over five million Instagram creators.

title: Beauty Brands Need 1,000-Creator Programs to Compete on TikTok Shop in 2026, Report Says
blurb: Beauty brands now need to seed 1,000 creators per quarter to stay visible on TikTok Shop, up from 100, as more than 30,000 brands compete for a category that drives 22.5% of the platform's global GMV. TikTok Shop GMV grew 94% year-over-year and is projected to hit $87B globally in 2026, and 5W's playbook says programs below 500 creators per quarter are structurally underscaled. The prescribed 90-day stack runs 750-plus micro-creators, 200-plus mid-tier, 30-plus dermfluencers, and 10-plus mega creators, with seeding returning roughly 3x ROI within 90 days.

title: Sprout Social Adds Snapchat Publishing Integration, Enabling Brands to Schedule Stories and Spotlights
blurb: Sprout Social added a Snapchat publishing integration across all plans, letting brands schedule and auto-publish Stories and Spotlights from the same calendar as their other channels, with AI Assist generating caption suggestions tuned to brand voice. The module also tracks performance across Public Stories, Saved Stories, and Spotlights and runs Snapchat influencer campaigns in one view, giving teams a way to reach younger audiences who open the app 30-plus times a day.

title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
blurb: Stratechery published an interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field, whose company IPO'd in 2025 at a $56.3B valuation but has since fallen below $10B on a market narrative that Figma is an AI loser. Field argues the opposite, framing AI as a tailwind and pointing to Config, where he positioned Figma's canvas as the natural meeting point between design and AI workflows.

title: The AI era requires a different kind of experimentation.
blurb: The author argues that the experimentation playbook most product teams built in 2022 is now obsolete, because AI has collapsed the build cycles that justified small, incremental A/B tests aimed at fast revenue wins. She makes the case for shifting toward bigger bets, testing foundational complexity and monetization rather than superficial surfaces, and accepting that some changes are now better shipped without a test at all.

title: Meta Relaunches Facebook Creator Studio as AI-Powered App
blurb: Meta relaunched Facebook Creator Studio as a standalone AI-powered app that learns a creator's voice and audience on first open, surfaces daily priorities, and drafts high-priority comment replies for review. Meta is also splitting its Professional Dashboard into separate Creator and Business products and adding Facebook creators to Creator Marketplace, signaling a push to consolidate creator tooling against rivals competing for the same audience.

title: Google Finance exits beta with a dedicated Android app, portfolio tracking, and AI-powered scheduled briefings
blurb: Google Finance exited beta with a dedicated Android app, portfolio tracking, an AI research tool, and scheduled briefings, restoring the portfolio feature it cut in 2017 with Gemini layered on top. The portfolio dashboard ingests CSVs, PDFs, or screenshots and lets the AI answer holdings-specific questions like which sectors are underweight, while natural-language scheduled briefings run in the background, with iOS arriving later this year.

title: Notion shutting down its AI-powered email client, including Mac and iOS apps
blurb: Notion is shutting down Notion Mail across web, desktop, and iOS on September 22, just over a year after launching the AI-powered email client in April 2025. The company says more than half of Mail users already manage email without opening their inbox, so it is redirecting effort toward agents that run the inbox rather than a standalone client.

title: Circle and Nomura join forces to target a $440 billion daily foreign exchange market in Japan
blurb: Circle and Nomura partnered to launch a USDC-based cross-border settlement and corporate payment service in Japan, targeting a foreign exchange market that handled $440B in daily transactions in 2025. The service, slated for as early as 2027, follows the Financial Services Agency clearing USDC as the first global dollar stablecoin approved for local corporate use, with Nomura handling onboarding and compliance.

title: Uniswap, Spark aim to build stablecoin FX market as banks, fintechs enter the industry
blurb: Spark and Uniswap are building a shared "FX layer" for stablecoins, a cross-issuer liquidity network that lets capital move between tokens and earn yield while idle, starting with a $150M migration of USDS, USDT, and PYUSD liquidity to Uniswap v4. The bet is that as bank- and fintech-issued stablecoins multiply, the liquidity routing layer between issuers becomes the contested infrastructure rather than the tokens themselves.

title: Spark, Uniswap build stablecoin ‘FX Layer’ seeded with $150 million liquidity migration
blurb: Spark and Uniswap are building a stablecoin "FX Layer" on Uniswap v4 to facilitate on-chain foreign-exchange swaps between stablecoins, seeded by migrating $150 million from Spark's USDS ecosystem into the new pool. The liquidity foundation aims to establish deep stablecoin-to-stablecoin pairs, giving DeFi traders tighter spreads for converting between dollar- and other currency-pegged tokens.

title: SBI Holdings agrees to acquire Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank in $288.6 million deal
blurb: SBI Holdings agreed to acquire Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank for 46.7 billion yen ($288.6 million), making it a wholly owned subsidiary through SBICAH LLC once the Japan Fair Trade Commission clears the deal around October 2026. Combining Bitbank with SBI VC Trade would push the group's crypto customer assets to roughly 1.1 trillion yen ($6.8 billion) across 2.92 million accounts, making SBI the largest domestic exchange operator by assets under management.

title: Kraken expands OTC lending through an onchain warehouse facility with Maple
blurb: Kraken partnered with decentralized lender Maple Finance to build what the two describe as the first fully onchain warehouse facility for digital asset-backed loans, supplying USDC liquidity to Kraken's institutional OTC borrowers who post BTC, ETH, and other crypto as collateral. Maple provides senior financing through a bankruptcy-remote SPV while Kraken acts as originator and junior lender, taking the first-loss position.

title: Four arrested in Poland over crypto SIM-swap attacks; ZachXBT links ‘Merry’ to case
blurb: Polish authorities arrested four members of an organized crime group accused of running SIM-swap attacks against cryptocurrency exchange accounts, draining user funds, and laundering proceeds estimated at tens of millions of zlotys, in an operation backed by the FBI and HSI. Onchain investigator ZachXBT linked one detainee to social-engineering actor Wojtek Kulisz, known as "Merry," citing designer items in raid footage matching his Instagram.

title: CoinEx denies claims it served as $3.84 billion gateway to sanctioned Iranian crypto firms
blurb: Blockchain intelligence firm TRM Labs said CoinEx routed more than $3.84 billion in traced flows to sanctioned Iranian crypto entities over seven years, including roughly $2.7 billion with Nobitex, the country's largest exchange recently sanctioned by the US Treasury. CoinEx denied any commercial relationship with Iranian exchanges or government bodies, arguing that onchain fund movement does not imply platform involvement, and said it has begun exiting Iran-related business.

title: Sophon sunsets its Layer 2 blockchain, moves to Base to build consumer apps
blurb: Sophon is shutting down its Layer 2 blockchain and migrating to Base to build consumer apps, cutting roughly $3M in annual chain-maintenance burn after raising $70M to date. The SOPH token, formerly the chain's gas token, shifts to a buyback-and-burn model tied to product revenue, with the first app, gamified neofinance product Pyre, launching early next month.

title: Bitcoin rout leads Strategy’s STRC to slide 26% below par as MSTR shares hits 16-month low
blurb: Strategy's STRC preferred stock slid as much as 26% below its $100 par value to a record low on Thursday, while MSTR common shares hit their lowest level since February 2024 as bitcoin briefly returned to $58,000. With annualized preferred dividend obligations near $1.2B against roughly $1.4B in cash reserves, scrutiny has shifted from bitcoin's price to whether Strategy can cover STRC's 11.5% dividend without pausing purchases.

title: ‘Stablecoin’ Google searches down 54% as supply growth stalls after 2025 boom
blurb: Google search volume for "stablecoins" fell roughly 54% month-over-month in June, dropping to an annualized reading near 45 from May's 98 and August 2025's all-time peak of 100. The slide tracks a stalling supply: aggregate stablecoin float peaked just under $300B in early June before slipping $5B, leaving total supply up only 0.23% year to date versus 46% growth in 2025.

title: Tether-pegged USDT0 stablecoin crosses $100 billion transaction volume milestone
blurb: USDT0, an omnichain version of Tether's USDT built on LayerZero's Omnichain Fungible Token standard, crossed $100 billion in cumulative transaction volume roughly 530 days after its January 2025 launch. About $4.1 billion USDT0 is in circulation across 23 native chain integrations, with its largest deployments on Arbitrum, Polygon, and the Tether-backed Plasma blockchain.

title: Kraken in talks to buy 15% stake in DeFi lender Aave at $385 million valuation
blurb: Kraken is in talks to buy a 15% equity stake in Aave at a $385 million valuation, investing 35,000 ETH for 250,000 AAVE tokens and common equity, with plans to syndicate the roughly $71 million deal. The move follows April's KelpDAO exploit, which saddled Aave with bad debt and triggered billions in withdrawals despite its smart contracts staying intact.

title: Magic Internet Money Falls 50% Below Peg as Abracadabra Declares Emergency
blurb: Abracadabra.money declared emergency measures after Magic Internet Money (MIM), its dollar-pegged stablecoin, fell to roughly $0.48, its worst sustained depeg on record. The protocol is raising interest rates across all Cauldrons to force debt repayment and contract MIM supply, having already deployed 70 million SPELL tokens and seeded a Curve pool as TVL collapsed to $5.11 million across Arbitrum and Ethereum.

title: Coinbase-incubated Base suffers mainnet chain stall interrupting block production
blurb: Base, Coinbase's incubated Ethereum L2 and the most active Ethereum scaling solution by activity, suffered a major mainnet outage on Thursday after a problematic block interrupted subsequent block building, halting reliable block production around 16:03 UTC. Deposits, withdrawals, and transactions were delayed or stalled, and the incident landed the same day as Base's scheduled Beryl upgrade, though it appears unrelated to the planned maintenance.

title: Michael Saylor’s Misfiring Funding Model Jolts Bitcoin Traders
blurb: Concerns that Michael Saylor's debt-and-equity-funded Bitcoin buying model is stalling rippled across crypto markets, deepening Bitcoin's latest selloff and exposing strain in one of the structures that has driven demand for two years. Strategy's playbook of raising capital to accumulate BTC depends on premium-to-NAV financing, and a weakening share price tightens its ability to keep buying.

title: Strategy's STRC Preferred Stock Hits Record Lows as Leverage Cascade Deepens
blurb: Strategy's STRC preferred shares hit a fresh all-time low this week, trading in the $73-78 range and extending a roughly 25% drawdown from their $100 par value in under two weeks. Analysts attribute the slide to a leverage liquidation cascade rather than weakened fundamentals, with the discount pushing STRC's effective yield to about 15-16% against Strategy's 847,363 BTC reserve and stated 32-year dividend coverage.

title: a16z-backed crypto firm rebrands, shifts focus to solving AI’s global copyright headache
blurb: Story Protocol rebranded as DATA Foundation and pivoted entirely from general intellectual property to blockchain infrastructure that verifies the provenance, licensing, and consent history of AI training data, following $140M in cumulative funding led by a16z crypto. The DATA Network issues cryptographic receipts documenting dataset origins and payment history without exposing the underlying data, and its launch integration with Kled AI registers 1.1 billion user-contributed records onchain.

title: Bitcoin tumbles to new multi-year low of $58,000, but a short-squeeze setup emerges
blurb: Bitcoin fell 5% to $58,000 in early Thursday US trading, its lowest level since 2024, before bouncing to $59,400 as ETH dropped 5.5% and the broader market sold off. Derivatives data points to a crowded short setup: open interest rose even as price fell, funding rates turned negative, and order-book depth shows $409M in bids below the market against $93M in resting sells.

title: UK fintech’s stablecoin clash with the Bank of England
blurb: The Bank of England softened its proposed stablecoin rules, scrapping the £20,000 individual holding cap in favor of a £40bn issuance cap and letting issuers back up to 70% of held sterling with gilts instead of interest-free central bank accounts. The reversal lets UK issuers earn yield on reserves and lets consumers hold larger balances, addressing industry warnings that the original framework would leave British stablecoins uncompetitive.

title: Half of UK wealth advisors say most clients’ crypto sits outside their oversight as 61% of European peers face firm restrictions: CoinShares
blurb: A CoinShares survey found 52% of UK wealth advisors report that most of their clients' crypto exposure sits outside their oversight, with firm policy cited as a key driver. The gap is wider on the continent, where 61% of European advisors face firm-level restrictions on crypto, leaving client holdings in self-custody or on exchanges beyond regulated advisory channels.

title: Strategy has a 10-month cash runway for dividends, but retail investors are losing faith
blurb: Strategy's MSTR fell 8% to $86 on Thursday, its lowest since February 2024, while its STRC perpetual preferred stock dropped to $75, a 25% discount to its $100 par. The company still holds enough dollar reserves to cover dividends for nearly 10 months, so the damage is to investor confidence rather than solvency, with mNAV compressing to 1.05.

title: Kraken sues crypto derivatives firm PowerTrade over 'misappropriated' funds claim
blurb: Kraken parent company Payward sued derivatives platform PowerTrade, alleging the UAE-based firm misappropriated more than $6 million of its digital assets by retroactively canceling settled trades to manufacture a negative account balance. Payward has filed an application with a US federal court seeking discovery from American financial institutions to support its case against PowerTrade and co-founders Mario Gomez Lozada and Bernd Sischka.

title: Kraken in talks to buy 15% stake in DeFi lender Aave at $385 million valuation
blurb: Kraken parent Payward is in talks to buy a 15% stake in DeFi lending protocol Aave at a $385 million valuation, investing 35,000 ETH for 250,000 AAVE tokens plus common equity. The deal would be the first in a series for Payward Asset Management's DeFi push, landing as Aave rebuilds from April's KelpDAO exploit that drained billions in deposits.

title: Circle and Nomura join forces to target a $440 billion daily foreign exchange market in Japan
blurb: Circle and Nomura partnered to launch a USDC-based settlement and corporate payment service in Japan as early as 2027, letting businesses swap yen for the stablecoin to cut cross-border FX transfers from days to minutes. The deal follows Japan's Financial Services Agency clearing USDC for local corporate use under updated payment rules, with Nomura handling onboarding, compliance, and integration into existing banking systems.

title: Coinbase-incubated Base suffers mainnet chain stall interrupting block production
blurb: Base, Coinbase's Ethereum layer-2 and the most active scaling solution by activity, suffered a major mainnet outage on Thursday after a problematic block stalled subsequent block production, halting reliable block creation and delaying deposits, withdrawals, and transactions. The disruption hit hours before Base's scheduled Beryl upgrade, underscoring the reliability risk operators inherit when settlement and payment flows depend on a single sequencer.

title: Uniswap, Spark aim to build stablecoin FX market as banks, fintechs enter the industry
blurb: Spark and Uniswap are building a shared "FX layer" for stablecoins, starting with a $150M liquidity migration to Uniswap v4 that pools USDS, USDT, and PYUSD so capital can move between issuers and earn yield while idle. The bet is that as banks, fintechs, and payment firms rush to issue their own stablecoins, the liquidity layer connecting them, not the coins themselves, becomes the contested infrastructure.

title: Spark, Uniswap build stablecoin ‘FX Layer’ seeded with $150 million liquidity migration
blurb: Spark and Uniswap are building a stablecoin "FX Layer" on Uniswap v4, seeded by migrating $150M from Spark's USDS ecosystem to anchor on-chain liquidity for stablecoin currency swaps. The setup positions Uniswap v4 hooks as settlement infrastructure for stablecoin-to-stablecoin FX, an area where slippage and depth have limited larger payment and treasury flows.

title: SBI Holdings agrees to acquire Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank in $288.6 million deal
blurb: SBI Holdings agreed to acquire Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank for 46.7 billion yen ($288.6 million), making it a wholly owned subsidiary pending Japan Fair Trade Commission clearance and a close expected around October 2026. Combining Bitbank with SBI VC Trade would push the group's crypto customer assets to roughly $6.8 billion across 2.92 million accounts, making SBI the largest domestic exchange operator by assets under management.

title: Kraken expands OTC lending through an onchain warehouse facility with Maple
blurb: Kraken partnered with decentralized lender Maple to build an onchain warehouse facility backing its OTC lending program, where institutional clients borrow USDC against BTC, ETH, and other crypto collateral. Modeled on traditional asset-backed securities, Maple provides senior financing through a bankruptcy-remote SPV while Kraken acts as originator and junior lender, taking the first-loss position.

title: Health unicorn Alan raises €480m Series G round led by Prosus
blurb: Alan raised €480m in a Series G led by Prosus, valuing the AI-native digital health insurer at €5.5bn alongside existing backers Index Ventures and Teachers' Venture Growth. The company reached over €800m in ARR in Q1 2026, up 53% year-on-year, serves 1.1m people, and is profitable in France, with the round earmarked for international expansion pending regulatory approval.

title: CoinEx denies claims it served as $3.84 billion gateway to sanctioned Iranian crypto firms
blurb: TRM Labs said CoinEx facilitated more than $3.84 billion in blockchain-traced flows with sanctioned Iranian crypto entities over seven years, including roughly $2.7 billion with Nobitex and exposure to wallets tied to the IRGC, weeks after the US Treasury sanctioned an array of Iranian exchanges. CoinEx rejected the findings, saying it holds no commercial relationship with Iranian exchanges or government entities and has begun exiting Iran-related business.

title: N26 hits profitability following leadership shakeup
blurb: N26 reported its first full year of net profitability in 2025, posting €1.6m net income against a €42m loss in 2024 as revenue grew 13% to €501.6m. The turnaround follows a leadership overhaul that ousted both founder co-CEOs and ongoing BaFin sanctions, including December limits on its lending business and new Netherlands operations that constrain how fast the neobank can grow in 2026.

title: Airwallex worth $16 billion after $460 million Series H
blurb: Airwallex raised $460M in a Series H led by existing backer Addition, lifting the Melbourne-founded fintech's valuation 37% to $16B and pushing annualized revenue to US$1.3B, up 74% year-over-year. The raise lands while AUSTRAC, Australia's financial-crimes regulator, investigates Airwallex over alleged AML/CTF compliance failures, even as the company directs the capital toward agentic-commerce products and expansion into new regulatory markets.

title: Bitcoin rout leads Strategy’s STRC to slide 26% below par as MSTR shares hits 16-month low
blurb: Strategy's STRC perpetual preferred stock fell as much as 26% below its $100 par value to a record low on Thursday, while MSTR common shares hit a 16-month low as bitcoin sank to $58,000. With annualized preferred dividend obligations near $1.2B against roughly $1.4B in cash, investor scrutiny has shifted from bitcoin's price to whether Saylor's preferred-share financing engine can cover its 11.5% STRC payouts.

title: JPMorgan names Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh co-presidents as longtime exec Marianne Lake exits
blurb: JPMorgan named Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh co-presidents and heads of its two largest divisions, while 25-year veteran and longtime CEO contender Marianne Lake is retiring. The move sharpens Jamie Dimon's succession picture, positioning one of the two men as the most likely heir and handing Rohrbaugh consumer banking to broaden a career built in institutional trading.

title: N26 hits first full-year profit, amid challenging 2025
blurb: N26 reported its first full-year profit, posting €1.6m in net income for 2025 against a €42m loss in 2024, as revenue rose 13% to €501.6m on higher card volumes, subscriptions, and cost cuts. The turnaround lands after a turbulent year that saw co-founders Stalf and Tayenthal exit, new CEO Mike Dargan take over, and fresh BaFin sanctions over compliance failures.

title: Michael Saylor’s Misfiring Funding Model Jolts Bitcoin Traders
blurb: Strategy's premium-to-NAV financing model, which let Michael Saylor issue equity and convertible debt to fund continuous Bitcoin purchases, is showing strain as the stock trades closer to the value of its underlying holdings. The squeeze removes a steady, leveraged source of Bitcoin demand that has supported prices for two years, accelerating the current selloff and raising questions about how levered treasury vehicles hold up in a downturn.

title: French health insurance outfit Alan agrees €480M funding round
blurb: Alan agreed a €480M Series G that values the French health-insurance startup at €5.5B, led by new investor Prosus alongside existing backers Index Ventures and Teachers' Venture Growth. The round, which includes secondary share purchases, follows a €100M raise months earlier and lands as Alan reports over €800M in ARR and 1.1M customers across France, Spain, Belgium, and Canada.

title: Taktile raises $110M to automate banking’s riskiest calls
blurb: Taktile raised $110M in a Series C led by Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives, with Balderton, Index, Tiger Global, and Y Combinator joining, bringing total funding to $184M. The Berlin- and New York-based startup sells an Agentic Decision Platform that automates underwriting, claims, onboarding, and AML checks for customers including Mercury, Monzo, and Pleo, claiming 95% automation in B2B underwriting and a 75% drop in false AML alerts.

title: ‘Stablecoin’ Google searches down 54% as supply growth stalls after 2025 boom
blurb: Google search volume for "stablecoin" fell to an annualized 45 in June from 98 in May and a record 100 in August 2025, a 54% monthly drop that tracks aggregate stablecoin supply slipping $5B from its ~$300B peak. Total supply is up just 0.23% year to date versus 46% growth in 2025, suggesting the GENIUS Act retail wave has fully onboarded and the next catalyst is Q4 bank-issued competition for USDT and USDC float.

title: SpaceX Credit Derivatives Start to Trade After Debut Bond Sale
blurb: Credit-default swaps tied to SpaceX have begun actively trading after the company sold high-grade bonds this week in its debut bond sale, giving investors a way to hedge or speculate on its creditworthiness. The arrival of a CDS market signals SpaceX is now a full participant in institutional credit, where dealer pricing of default risk becomes an independent read on the privately held company's financial health.

title: Tether-pegged USDT0 stablecoin crosses $100 billion transaction volume milestone
blurb: USDT0, the LayerZero-based omnichain version of Tether's USDT, crossed $100 billion in cumulative transaction volume roughly 530 days after its January 2025 launch, with about $4.1 billion now in circulation. Backed 1:1 by USDT and natively integrated on 23 chains, the token is spreading stablecoin liquidity across Arbitrum, Polygon, and Tether's own Plasma chain rather than concentrating it on a single network.

title: Bitcoin tumbles to new multi-year low of $58,000, but a short-squeeze setup emerges
blurb: Bitcoin plunged 5% to a multi-year low of $58,000 on Thursday, its weakest level since 2024, before bouncing to $59,400 as the selloff spread to ETH and SOL. Derivatives data points to a crowded short setup, with rising open interest, negative funding rates, and 6,900 BTC ($409M) in resting bids below the market against just 1,570 BTC in sell orders, conditions that often precede a short squeeze.

title: UK fintech’s stablecoin clash with the Bank of England
blurb: The Bank of England eased its proposed sterling stablecoin rules, scrapping a £20,000 per-holder cap in favor of a £40bn issuance ceiling and letting issuers back up to 70% of reserves with gilts rather than non-interest-bearing central bank accounts. The reversal followed industry warnings that overly restrictive limits would leave UK stablecoins uncompetitive, and it hands issuers a path to profit on float while letting consumers hold larger balances.

title: Half of UK wealth advisors say most clients’ crypto sits outside their oversight as 61% of European peers face firm restrictions: CoinShares
blurb: A CoinShares survey found 52% of UK wealth advisors report that most clients' crypto exposure sits outside their oversight, with 61% of European peers operating under firm-level restrictions on advising about digital assets. The gap signals that client crypto holdings are flowing through channels traditional wealth managers can't monitor, leaving advisors exposed on suitability and compliance even as demand pulls allocations onto their books.

title: Data Center Surge Brings Risk for States and Munis, Moody’s Says
blurb: Moody's Ratings warned that the surge in data center construction is creating credit risks for states and municipalities, as the projects strain local power grids and water supplies. The concentration of demand from a single industry exposes municipal bond issuers to revenue volatility if AI buildouts slow, leaving governments holding the cost of infrastructure upgrades made for tenants that may not stay.

title: JPM’s Lake Exits, Resetting the Race to Succeed Dimon
blurb: JPMorgan named markets co-head Troy Rohrbaugh and commercial-banking chief Doug Petno as co-presidents following the retirement of consumer banking head Marianne Lake, narrowing the field of internal candidates to eventually succeed CEO Jamie Dimon. The reshuffle elevates two executives from the markets and commercial-banking side, signaling those franchises now weigh as heavily as consumer banking in the bank's long-running succession planning.

title: Airwallex raises $320M at $11B valuation to let AI run your finance team from day one
blurb: Airwallex raised $320M in a Series H round at an $11B valuation, up from $8B six months ago, with the capital funding new AI products T:0 and Airi that aim to automate finance functions and agentic payments. The raise lands on $1.3B in annualized revenue as of March 2026, up 74% year-on-year, and reframes the company from a cross-border payments platform into an AI-native financial operating system.
